sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, end_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, timestamp '2021-12-31 18:40:40', timestamp '2021-12-31 16:30:20');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (2, timestamp '2021-12-31 00:00:00', timestamp NULL);
SELECT id, timestampdiff_min(start_timestamp, end_timestamp) | (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates) AS calculated_diff FROM test_dates WHERE start_timestamp IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (timestampdiff_min(start_timestamp, end_timestamp) + 5) | 2 AS final_result FROM test_dates WHERE end_timestamp IS NOT NULL ORDER BY final_result;
CREATE TABLE other_table (other_id INT, data VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO other_table VALUES (1, 'Test Data 1'), (2, 'Test Data 2');

SELECT CASE WHEN end_timestamp IS NOT NULL THEN timestampdiff_min(start_timestamp, end_timestamp) ELSE NULL END AS duration, (other_id | 2) AS adjusted_id FROM test_dates JOIN other_table ON end_timestamp IS NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS cnt, SUM(timestampdiff_min(start_timestamp, end_timestamp)) | MAX(other_id) AS agg_val FROM test_dates JOIN other_table ON other_id = 1 GROUP BY id HAVING cnt > 1;

SELECT id, timestampdiff_min(start_timestamp, (SELECT MIN(start_timestamp) FROM test_dates)) | 3 AS adjusted_difference FROM test_dates WHERE start_timestamp > '2021-01-01 00:00:00' ORDER BY adjusted_difference ASC;
